name: Nabila Alia Binti Hazman

highlights.text: Final Year Project: Simulated phishing campaigns using Gophish to analyze security awareness and risk.
Academic Project: Optimized campus Wi-Fi performance through router configuration and network redesign.

bullets.text: Facilitated digital migration from Google Workspace to Microsoft 365 for a dedicated user group, verifying access and resolving post-migration issues.
Authored comprehensive user guides to streamline the transition to new productivity tools and minimize support requests.
Managed the incident lifecycle within a ticket-based workflow, triaging user-reported problems and escalating critical risks to engineers.
Prepared technical audit evidence for ISO/IEC 27001:2022 and WebTrust CA reviews to support organizational compliance goals.
Executed security configuration checks using SCAP scans and CIS Benchmarks to identify potential system vulnerabilities.
Monitored enterprise security alerts using Wazuh SIEM, reviewing system logs to ensure operational integrity.
Coordinated with cross-functional teams and auditors to clarify technical findings and fulfill operational requirements.
Monitored network infrastructure including routers, switches, and firewalls using Wireshark and SNMP tools.
Diagnosed network performance issues by analyzing traffic patterns and logs to ensure stable connectivity.
Documented network availability trends and prepared clear technical reports for internal management reviews.

certifications: CCNA: Enterprise Networking, Security, and Automation – Cisco
CCNAv7: Switching, Routing, and Wireless Essentials – Cisco
Junior Cybersecurity Analyst Career Path – Cisco
Google IT Automation with Python – In Progress
